overview: This week I upgraded my SAFe Feature Spec System to production on Streamlit Cloud and ran a controlled experiment with Anthropic's new advisor tool, which lets Sonnet consult Opus mid-generation. After an encouraging Day 1 result, the full golden set showed the advisor averaged -2.2 points at 4.3× the cost, so I shipped the toggle but defaulted it OFF and redirected my attention to infrastructure work that made deployment possible.

keyPoints.title: Advisor tool averaged -2.2 points at 4.3× the cost across golden set
keyPoints.description: Full evaluation showed $4.98 vs $1.17 with no quality gain — the Day 1 +9 point result was an outlier, not a trend.
keyPoints.sectionName: Verdict on Advisor Tool: The Data Said No (At Least for My Use Cases)

keyPoints.title: End-to-end deployment scored 76→85, $0.72, 27 calls across 5 agents
keyPoints.description: I verified the live Streamlit Cloud URL by processing a queued request through the full pipeline with boost inputs and confirmed governance data written back to PostgreSQL.

keyPoints.title: Wired AuditTrail, CostGuard, and TokenTracker into the live pipeline
keyPoints.description: These modules existed since Weeks 9–10 but were disconnected; I connected all three to every agent call and added a flush guard to prevent duplicate database writes on Streamlit reruns.
keyPoints.sectionName: Building Guardrails Is Half the Work

keyPoints.title: Replaced SQLite with PostgreSQL across all six evaluation tables
keyPoints.description: I implemented dual-mode connection logic — PostgreSQL via environment variable in production, SQLite fallback locally — which unlocked concurrent access from the eval runner, request queue, and live pipeline.

keyPoints.title: Killed the Notion MCP integration and built a connector abstraction instead
keyPoints.description: OAuth requirements, stdio incompatibility with Streamlit Cloud, and bridge-process limitations stacked up, so I replaced the hard dependency with a connector interface that normalizes any intake source into a standard object.

keyPoints.title: Built a Request Queue page as an alternative pipeline entry point
keyPoints.description: I added a PostgreSQL-backed connector interface with create, process, and completed-request views, though I noted the UX needs significant improvement before a typical tech marketing manager could use it successfully.

keyPoints.title: Advisor tool will return in Week 12 for the Intake Copilot's ambiguous reasoning tasks
keyPoints.description: Structured rubric scoring was too well-defined for Opus consultation to help, but processing messy stakeholder input — where the model must decide what questions to ask — is the kind of open-ended task the advisor is actually designed for.
